hard.next_step: 6/11/26 MQ Sent Mandy a copy of our MNDA for review and execution. Scheduling internal pre-call review with SC and sending potential dates to Mandy. 6/10/26 MQ Initial call held on 6/9. Positioned follow up call for deep dive discovery/solution overview for iAnalyze, Merlin Intake, ANA and taxonomy offering. Getting SC assigned.
hard.ais_why:
• Owens & Minor's new Indirect Procurement head Mandy Huff is 'reviewing/streamlining current processes and evaluating tools to plug functional gaps,' currently focused on spend analysis and taxonomy.
• ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped with 'Potential for Intake and ANA,' so agentic modules are candidates but not yet committed.
• No agentic discussion or AI-requirement is recorded; interest is process-gap driven, placing appetite mid-range.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early Qualified deal on cadence: strong 9 Jun 2026 discovery with new champion Mandy Huff, spend-analytics demo agreed, close 243 days out on 28 Feb 2027; buyer has gone quiet since the call while the rep chases the MNDA.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Jun 2026, qualified 10 Jun 2026, ~27 days in Qualified. Last verified activity 23 Jun 2026 (rep-side MNDA chase). Last genuine two-way buyer touch was the 9 Jun 2026 intro call; since then all activity is rep-initiated email (awaiting Mandy's reply on dates and MNDA). Close 28 Feb 2027 is 243 days out, ample runway for an early-stage enterprise cycle. No slip required; pace matches a deal opened four weeks ago.
evidence: Discovery/intro call held 9 Jun 2026 with Mandy Huff (Head of Indirect Procurement); Marc Quessenberry agreed a follow-up spend-analytics and Spinminer demo (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026).
Forecast category Pipeline honestly matches a Qualified-stage deal 243 days from close (SF ForecastCategory).

Rep-side outreach only since the call: MNDA sent 11 Jun 2026, dates and Horizon invite emailed 11-17 Jun, MNDA-execution task still open 23 Jun 2026 (SF tasks) — rep reached out, awaiting Mandy's reply.

metrics.narrative: Mandy Huff articulated clear qualitative pain but no quantified value case yet. She described a 140-year-old company grown through acquisition with 'a lot of legacy, smaller suppliers' across '45 DCs just in The United States' that 'like to operate independently', driving tail/maverick spend and poor visibility (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). She framed centralizing indirect spend as how 'we get your value... our protection... deleverage and eliminate risk'. No cost, savings, headcount, or cycle-time figures were stated, so C-level budget justification is not yet supportable. Quantifying tail-spend leakage across the 45 DCs is a ranked next move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is inferred but not yet named or engaged. Mandy Huff said indirect orchestration 'doesn't sit directly under my responsibility' but that she has 'aligned with my CPO' to have these conversations and make introductions, and floated inviting 'potentially my CPO' to Horizon (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). This points to Owens & Minor's CPO as the budget owner for a $300k platform purchase at a Platinum-Equity-owned enterprise, but the CPO is unnamed on the call and carries no Salesforce contact record on this opportunity, so no name can be asserted. Getting Mandy to introduce the CPO is a ranked move.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging, not formalized. Mandy said she wants 'to understand more about your orchestration' and Merlin Intake, and that 'understanding a little bit more about your spend analytics' is her focus area (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). She flagged taxonomy help ('I've not worked for a company that doesn't need help with their taxonomy'), contract-management automation ('more of a manual process right now'), and tail-spend negotiation on renewals. These map directly to the in-scope products (ML iAnalyze/Spinminer, Merlin Intake, ANA). No formal RFP or weighted criteria exist yet; the agreed demo is the venue to confirm them.

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is undefined this early. The only process signal is Mandy's stated intent to 'have these conversations and have some introductions' and to 'loop others in' as conversations continue, plus alignment with her CPO (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). No procurement steps, approval chain, evaluation stages, or timeline have been described, and the post-acquisition Platinum Equity reorganization means the process itself is in flux. The call sequence so far is a single 9 Jun discovery with an SC-led demo agreed next; mapping the decision and approval process is a near-term move once the demo lands.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is at its earliest point: an MNDA was sent 11 Jun 2026 for review and execution and is still outstanding as of 6 Jul 2026 (SF Next Step note 11 Jun 2026; open MNDA task 23 Jun 2026). Mandy explicitly flagged the absence of an NDA on the call as a reason to be 'careful with specifics' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026), so executing the MNDA directly unblocks deeper discovery and competitive-intel sharing. No MSA, SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec path has been discussed yet — expected this early.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated and tied to a real compelling event. Owens & Minor was 'acquired by the relevant stakeholder in January' with a subsequent business separation, driving 'change... ways of working and the sense of urgency' and a mandate to 'revisit how we're doing things... be more nimble... reevaluate all of our agreements' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026, Mandy Huff). Specific pains: rogue/maverick spend across '45 DCs' that 'operate independently', weak spend visibility and messy taxonomy from growth-by-acquisition, and manual, underutilized contract management ('we can have all the technology we want, but if we're not using it'). The pain owner is Mandy as Head of Indirect Procurement, tasked since April 2026 with centralizing indirect spend.

champion.narrative: Mandy Huff is a developing champion. She recalled Zycus positively from the 2019 Horizon conference in Charlotte, engaged openly on the 9 Jun call, and said she has 'aligned with my CPO' and wants introductions and to 'loop others in' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). Her advocacy is genuine but unproven internally: she is new to the role (joined April 2026), candidly said 'I'm not sure what the opportunity would be here', and orchestration 'doesn't sit directly under my responsibility'. What has developed: an engaged senior procurement leader who pulled for a spend-analytics demo. What is missing: evidence she can mobilize budget or the buying committee, and any second thread. Equipping her with a quantified tail-spend story and multi-threading to her CPO and CoE team is a ranked move.

competition.narrative: No named competitor is in active evaluation. Owens & Minor runs an unnamed incumbent S2P and contract-management platform that Mandy called 'a pretty substantial procurement tool' but described as underused and manual ('I don't think we do use it to its full capability... it's more of a manual process right now'), and she said full S2P 'is not something that would change in the near future' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). The rep referenced intake-only players generically ('there's other companies like Zips out there... all they do is intake') as a contrast, not a buyer-evaluated rival. The real contest here is inertia and idle incumbent capability, not a competitor actively winning; the wedge is proving differentiated value on spend analytics, taxonomy clean-up, Teams-embedded Merlin Intake, and ANA negotiation alongside the incumbent.

deal_movement.summary: Deal opened 9 Jun 2026 and qualified next day; a single strong discovery call is the only two-way buyer touch. Since then the rep has driven MNDA, dates, and a Horizon invite by email while awaiting Mandy's reply; field-history shows only internal recategorization (Division, Source) on 29 Jun 2026.

items.change: Opportunity created and owner set to Marc Quessenberry
Intro/discovery call held with Mandy Huff; spend-analytics demo agreed
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified; Type set to New Business
MNDA sent to Mandy for legal review and execution
Proposed meeting dates and Horizon 2026 invite emailed to Mandy
MNDA-execution follow-up task opened; still awaiting buyer response
Internal recategorization: Division to New Markets, Source to Linkedin PPC

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor is in active evaluation as of 6 Jul 2026. The only competitive factor is an unnamed incumbent S2P and contract-management platform Mandy Huff described as substantial but underutilized and manual (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026); the current threat is low and the displacement angle is the incumbent's idle capability plus status-quo inertia, not a rival actively winning. The single most useful next step is naming the incumbent and its adoption/satisfaction during the demo. 'Zips' (Zip) was named only by the rep as a generic intake-only contrast, not a buyer-evaluated rival, so it is not ranked as a competitor.

competitors.name: Unnamed incumbent S2P platform
competitors.quote: We have a pretty substantial procurement tool... we have something for S2P... that's not something that would change in the near future. That's a huge undertaking. (Mandy Huff, call 2026-06-09)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as a modular best-of-breed spend-analytics/intake/taxonomy layer that integrates alongside the incumbent S2P (1,121 APIs), not a rip-and-replace; lead with embedded-AI differentiation (Merlin agentic platform, Teams-embedded intake, ANA autonomous negotiation) and prove ROI on tail-spend visibility and taxonomy clean-up in a no-cost pilot. Identify the incumbent on the demo and tailor the integration story.

competitors.name: Unnamed incumbent contract-management platform
competitors.quote: We have contract management right now... I don't think we do use it to its full capability. We have it's more of a manual process right now. (Mandy Huff, call 2026-06-09)
competitors.how_we_win: Prove embedded-AI breadth beats idle incumbent capability: Merlin Intake in Teams (already pervasive at O&M), iAnalyze/Spinminer for tail-spend visibility, and ANA for renewal negotiations on a single same-code-base platform versus bolted-on AI.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Mandy's stated interests map cleanly to the in-scope products, but her expectations are exploratory and hedged ('I'm not sure what the opportunity would be here'), and full S2P replacement is explicitly out of scope near-term.

items.criterion: Spend analytics and tail-spend visibility across 45 independent DCs
items.quote: The spend analysis is a big tool and a big enabler for me... understanding more around your spend offering would be where I would like to have a follow up call.

items.criterion: Taxonomy clean-up for a growth-by-acquisition supplier base
items.quote: I've not worked for or consulted for a company that doesn't need help with their taxonomy.
I wanna understand more about your orchestration... understanding what that looks like, I think, is helpful.
We have something for S2P... I think that's not something that would change in the near future. That's a huge undertaking.

items.requirement: Deliver a follow-up demo of spend analytics and Spinminer
items.quote: Understanding more around your spend offering would be where I would like to have a follow up call... the Spinminer is something I think you would be interested to in seeing as well.

items.deliverable: Send Mandy the executed MNDA format and Horizon 2026 invitation
items.grounding_quote: I'll get you an invitation now if you'd be interested... I'll send you a follow up email, and then we'll maybe get together and see if we can find some times.
items.source: Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026 + SF task 11 Jun 2026

items.deliverable: Have legal review and execute the Zycus MNDA and return availability for the demo
items.grounding_quote: Has your legal department started their review of our MNDA? If you can provide a couple of available time slots next week, I will coordinate.
items.source: SF Next Step note 11 Jun 2026 + email task 17 Jun 2026

items.quote: I've aligned with my CPO... maybe even potentially my CPO if he could make it.

items.area: Incumbent S2P vendor name and satisfaction
items.quote: We do have a pretty substantial procurement tool.

items.area: Decision process, evaluation stages, and timeline
items.quote: As conversations continue on, we're loop others in.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with one strong discovery call, single-threaded to a brand-new champion, no named competitor, and a clear post-acquisition compelling event to anchor urgency.
flags: Single-threaded to Mandy Huff, new since April 2026 and whose remit excludes orchestration; multi-thread to her CPO and CoE/ops team before or at the demo.
Pain is qualitative only; quantify tail-spend leakage across the 45 US DCs to build a C-level business case for the CPO.
No competitor is actively winning; the real threat is do-nothing inertia and idle incumbent tools, so anchor urgency to the Platinum Equity separation and agreement-reevaluation mandate.

items.name: Mandy Huff
items.title: Head of Indirect Procurement
items.risk: New to role (April 2026); internal influence and budget authority unproven; single thread; orchestration outside her direct remit; quiet since the 9 Jun call.
items.source: Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026 + SF task contact

items.name: Owens & Minor CPO (unnamed)
items.title: Chief Procurement Officer
items.risk: Budget owner Mandy has aligned with but not yet introduced; no Salesforce contact record, so unnamed and never engaged. Must be mapped and met to progress a $300k purchase.
items.source: Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026 (Mandy Huff, inferred budget owner)

champion_strength.summary: Mandy Huff is an engaged, positively-disposed champion who pulled for a spend-analytics demo and says she has aligned with her CPO, but she is new to her role (April 2026), candidly unsure of the opportunity, and orchestration sits outside her direct remit — advocacy is real but internal mobilizing power is unproven.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Marc led heavily with the Merlin agentic story (Teams-embedded intake, ANA autonomous negotiation, 32 AI patents, no-cost pilots), but Mandy stayed focused on spend analytics and taxonomy and did not engage the agentic narrative, so the AI story is being pushed harder than the buyer is pulling. The agentic modules (Merlin Intake, ANA) are scoped as candidates, not committed needs.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate is well-calibrated to the call: interest is process-gap driven (spend visibility, taxonomy, contract automation) rather than AI-led. Mandy recalled Zycus's 2019 AI messaging but raised no AI requirement and did not react to the Merlin/ANA agentic pitch, so appetite is genuine but mid-range and exploratory.

items.detail: Economic buyer (the CPO) is inferred but unnamed and unengaged; a $300k purchase at a PE-owned enterprise needs C-level sponsorship not yet secured.
Single-threaded to a champion new since April 2026 whose remit excludes orchestration and whose internal influence is unproven.
No quantified value case; pain is qualitative, so budget justification for the CPO is not yet supportable.
Post-acquisition flux plus a candid 'we can have all the technology we want, but if we're not using it' signals adoption/status-quo risk that could stall a decision.

confidence_signals.summary: One transcribed discovery call, single buyer contact, no reply since 9 Jun while the rep chases MNDA and dates. Momentum is rep-side, not buyer-side; the deal is live but the follow-up demo date is not yet locked.
items: Buyer pull: Mandy explicitly asked for a spend-analytics follow-up (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
Rep-side only since 9 Jun: MNDA, dates, and Horizon invite emailed 11-17 Jun with no buyer reply logged (SF tasks).
MNDA still unexecuted 23 Jun 2026, gating deeper discovery (SF task).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A fresh, well-run Qualified deal: Mandy Huff, Owens & Minor's new indirect-procurement head, pulled for a spend-analytics demo off a post-acquisition mandate, but she is a single, brand-new thread with no CPO engaged, no quantified case, and no reply since the 9 Jun call.

win_position.text: We are early but credibly positioned: Mandy Huff articulated clear post-acquisition pain (rogue spend across 45 DCs, messy taxonomy, manual contracts) and asked us back for a spend-analytics demo (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
No competitor is actively winning; the only rival is an unnamed incumbent S2P tool Mandy herself calls underused and manual, leaving room for a modular best-of-breed wedge (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
The economic buyer is only inferred: Mandy says she has 'aligned with my CPO' but the CPO is unnamed and has never been engaged on a $300k PE-owned purchase (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
We are single-threaded to a champion new since April 2026 whose remit excludes orchestration, and she candidly said 'I'm not sure what the opportunity would be here' (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Mandy pulled for the next meeting, requesting a spend-analytics and Spinminer follow-up demo (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
Since the 9 Jun call all movement is rep-side: MNDA, proposed dates, and a Horizon invite emailed 11-17 Jun with no buyer reply logged (SF tasks).
No customer-named dates or evaluation timeline exist yet, and the demo date is still unbooked (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

customer_commitment.text: Mandy shared candid internal context on the Platinum Equity separation and named taxonomy and tail-spend as priorities (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
The MNDA sent 11 Jun 2026 remains unexecuted as of 23 Jun 2026, gating deeper discovery and specifics (SF task).
No budget, procurement process, or CPO introduction has been committed; commitment is exploratory, not decisional (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing is the primary threat: Owens & Minor keeps running the incumbent tools it already underuses, a no-decision risk rather than a loss to a competitor (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
Full S2P replacement is explicitly off the table near-term, capping scope to spend analytics, intake, and taxonomy (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
Single-thread and an unmapped, unnamed economic buyer leave the deal exposed if Mandy's internal push stalls (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

concrete_dates.evidence: No customer-named dates; demo unbooked (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

economic_buyer_engaged.evidence: CPO inferred but unnamed and never engaged (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

customer_requested_next_meeting.evidence: Mandy asked for the spend-analytics follow-up (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

items.expected_effect: Unblocks deeper discovery, incumbent-vendor detail, and competitive-intel sharing.

customer_preference.evidence: Mandy asked deeper Zycus-specific questions on spend analytics, Spinminer, taxonomy, and orchestration and pulled for a follow-up demo, but hedged with 'I'm not sure what the opportunity would be here' (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (rogue spend across 45 DCs, taxonomy, manual contracts) but no cost, savings, or cycle-time figures stated (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).

contributions.evidence: Mandy asked for the spend-analytics follow-up (Avoma 9 Jun 2026).
Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 23 is carried by momentum 60 vs stage-expected 50; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 60 (moving forward): Mandy asked for the spend-analytics follow-up (Avoma 9 Jun 2026). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 16 is anchored on the stage win (23), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 60, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
